evidence_text: LoCoMo contains 50 very long-term dialogues, each averaging 300 turns and 9K tokens, over up to 35 sessions.
source_excerpt: "With this pipeline, we create LoCoMo, a dataset of 50 very long-term dialogues, each consisting of 300 turns and 9K tokens on avg., over up to 35 sessions."
note: Dataset-level descriptive statistic reported in the paper.
caveat: Dataset-level descriptive statistic reported in the paper.

evidence_text: In Table 1, LoCoMo averages 304.9 turns, 19.3 sessions, and 9,209.2 tokens per conversation, spans a few months, and is multimodal.
note: Table 1 reports comparative dataset statistics against prior dialogue datasets.
caveat: Table 1 reports comparative dataset statistics against prior dialogue datasets.

evidence_text: The data pipeline first generates conversations with LLM-based agents and then has human annotators fix long-term inconsistencies.
source_excerpt: "we first use LLM-based generative agents to generate conversations and then ask human annotators to fix any long-term inconsistencies in the conversations"
note: This is a pipeline description, not a performance claim.
caveat: This is a pipeline description, not a performance claim.

evidence_text: Each agent’s full persona is expanded with gpt-3.5-turbo from an initial 4 to 5 sentence persona statement selected from MSC.
source_excerpt: "We select an initial persona statement pc from the MSC dataset Xu et al. (2022), encompassing 4 to 5 sentences, and employ gpt-3.5-turbo as M to expand these into full persona statement p."
note: This describes persona construction for the generation pipeline.
caveat: This describes persona construction for the generation pipeline.

evidence_text: Each agent’s temporal event graph contains up to 25 dated events over 6 to 12 months, generated iteratively in batches of 3 with causal links between events.
source_excerpt: "For each G, we create up to 25 events, spread across a time frame of 6 to 12 months... Initially, a small batch of k = 3 events is generated, which is then used iteratively as input prompt to create the subsequent batch of k events."
note: This directly describes how long-range temporal structure is induced in the synthetic conversations.
caveat: This directly describes how long-range temporal structure is induced in the synthetic conversations.

evidence_text: The reflect-and-respond mechanism stores session summaries in short-term memory and turn-level observations in long-term memory for later response generation.
citation: Maharana et al., Section 3.3 Reflect & Respond
source_excerpt: "Following each session k, each agent is asked to produce a summary wk that is then stored in the short-term Hs... For each turn j within session k, a single turn of the conversation hkj is transformed into an observation okj and then stored in the long-term memory Hl."
note: This is a direct description of the memory mechanism used by the agents.
caveat: This is a direct description of the memory mechanism used by the agents.

evidence_text: Image sharing is implemented as caption generation, keyword generation, web image search, and image sharing; image reaction captions the received image with BLIP-2 and then generates a response.
citation: Maharana et al., Section 3.3 Image Sharing & Image Reaction
source_excerpt: "The image sharing function is called when the agent decides to send an image. This process includes: (1) Generate a caption c... (2) Convert the caption c into relevant keywords... (3) Use the keywords k to find an image through web search WEB(k); (4) Share the chosen image... Conversely, the image reaction function is triggered upon receiving an image... (1) Generate caption c for the received image... We use BLIP-2...; (2) Generate a reaction for the received image in response"
note: This directly describes the multimodal generation mechanism used in the pipeline.
caveat: This directly describes the multimodal generation mechanism used in the pipeline.

evidence_text: Human annotators edited nearly 15% of dialogue turns and removed or substituted about 19% of images in the LLM-generated dataset.
source_excerpt: "Overall, we observed that annotators edited nearly 15% of the dialog turns and removed or substituted approx. 19% images present in the LLM-generated dataset."
note: These edit rates indicate a substantial amount of post-generation correction remained necessary.
caveat: These edit rates indicate a substantial amount of post-generation correction remained necessary.

evidence_text: The QA benchmark contains 7,512 questions: 2,705 single-hop, 1,104 multi-hop, 1,547 temporal, 285 open-domain, and 1,871 adversarial.
source_excerpt: "QA Benchmark Statistics ... # questions. single-hop retrieval 2,705 (36%) ... # questions. multi-hop retrieval 1,104 (14.6%) ... # questions. temporal reasoning 1,547 (20.6%) ... # questions. open domain knowledge 285 (3.9%) ... # questions. adversarial 1,871 (24.9%) ... Total. # questions. 7,512"
note: This is the benchmark composition reported in Appendix B.1.
caveat: This is the benchmark composition reported in Appendix B.1.

evidence_text: Human overall QA F1 is 87.9, while the best reported long-context model in Table 2, gpt-3.5-turbo-16K at 16K context, reaches 37.8 overall.
source_excerpt: "Human Human - ... Overall 87.9" and "Long context GPT-3.5-turbo-16K ... 16K ... Overall 37.8"
note: This evidence preserves the human-model performance gap reported in the QA benchmark.
caveat: This evidence preserves the human-model performance gap reported in the QA benchmark.

evidence_text: For adversarial QA, gpt-3.5-turbo-16K drops to 2.1 F1 at 16K context, compared with 70.2 for GPT-4-turbo in the 4K base setting.
citation: Maharana et al., Table 2 and Section 6.1
source_excerpt: "Long context GPT-3.5-turbo-16K ... 16K ... Adversarial 2.1" and "GPT-4-turbo 4,096 ... Adversarial 70.2" and "its performance on adversarial questions drops to a mere 2.1%"
note: The source presents this as evidence that long-context exposure can coincide with strong adversarial vulnerability and hallucination.
caveat: The source presents this as evidence that long-context exposure can coincide with strong adversarial vulnerability and hallucination.

evidence_text: In RAG QA, using top-5 observations yields 41.4 overall F1, higher than top-5 dialog retrieval at 31.7 and top-5 summary retrieval at 32.5.
citation: Maharana et al., Table 3 and Section 6.1
note: This directly supports the paper’s claim that observation-based retrieval is the strongest reported RAG context among the listed top-5 settings.
caveat: This directly supports the paper’s claim that observation-based retrieval is the strongest reported RAG context among the listed top-5 settings.

evidence_text: Observation-based RAG declines from 41.4 overall F1 at top-5 retrieval to 37.8 at top-50 retrieval.
citation: Maharana et al., Table 3 and Section 6.1
source_excerpt: "Observation 5 ... Overall 41.4" and "Observation 50 ... Overall 37.8" and "This improvement falters with an increase in the number of retrieved observations, suggesting that it is important to reduce the signal-to-noise (SNR) ratio in retrieved contexts"
note: The source explicitly ties worse performance at larger retrieval sets to higher noise in retrieved context.
caveat: The source explicitly ties worse performance at larger retrieval sets to higher noise in retrieved context.

evidence_text: For event summarization, gpt-3.5-turbo with 4,096 context has the highest reported FactScore F1 at 45.9, while gpt-3.5-turbo-16K scores 39.9.
citation: Maharana et al., Table 4 and Section 6.2
source_excerpt: "GPT-3.5-turbo 4,096 ... FactScore ... F1 45.9" and "Long context GPT-3.5-turbo-16K 16K ... FactScore ... F1 39.9" and "the long-context model does not surpass the base model"
note: This preserves the reported contrast that a larger context window did not improve this summarization task.
caveat: This preserves the reported contrast that a larger context window did not improve this summarization task.

evidence_text: Manual analysis identifies five broad event-summarization error categories: missing information, hallucinations, misunderstanding dialog cues, inaccurate speaker attributions, and saliency errors.
citation: Maharana et al., Section 6.2 and Table 7
source_excerpt: "we identify five broad categories of event summarization errors made by LLMs: (1) missing information ... (2) hallucinations ... (3) errors from misunderstanding of dialog cues ... (4) inaccurate speaker attributions; and (5) insignificant dialogs that are wrongly considered as salient events."
note: This is an error taxonomy from manual analysis rather than a benchmark score.
caveat: This is an error taxonomy from manual analysis rather than a benchmark score.

evidence_text: In multimodal dialogue generation, MiniGPT-5 with top-5 retrieved observations achieves the highest reported MM-Relevance score of 57.8, above the base model’s 56.1, and the paper reports MM-Relevance drops as dialogue history grows.
citation: Maharana et al., Table 6 and Section 6.3
source_excerpt: "With this pipeline, we create LoCoMo, a dataset of 50 very long-term dialogues, each consisting of 300 turns and 9K tokens on avg., over up to 35 sessions."
note: Dataset-level descriptive statistic reported in the paper.
caveat: Dataset-level descriptive statistic reported in the paper.
